tool.name: mcp_resource
tool.description: Read a remote MCP server resource (by name or URI) and return its contents. Use this tool during MCP dynamic analysis to inspect configuration files, documentation, logs and other readonly data exposed as MCP resources.
tool.details:
This tool forwards the read request to the configured remote MCP server (configured via CLI --server_url).
- You may specify either `resource_name` (preferred) or `uri`. - If `resource_name` is provided, it will be resolved using the resource list discovered from the MCP server. - If both are provided, `uri` takes precedence. - The returned content can be plain text or base64-encoded binary data, depending on the resource type.

parameter.name: resource_name
parameter.description: Logical name of the MCP resource to read. This should match the `name` field from the `` listing.

parameter.name: uri
parameter.description: Full URI of the MCP resource to read. When provided, this takes precedence over `resource_name`.

returns.description: Response containing: - resource_name: Name used to look up the resource (if any) - uri: Final URI that was read from the MCP server - content: The resource contents (string or base64-encoded blob) - error: Error details if the remote read fails
